title: Rifleman - Normandy, June 1944
startLocation: Aboard LCI-94 in the English Channel, off Omaha Beach
opening: The Channel is rough and the landing craft smells of diesel, seawater, and three hundred seasick men. Nobody slept. Sergeant Kowalski moves down the packed deck checking gear by red flashlight - Mae West, rifle wrapped against the spray, the invasion francs they handed out like play money. 'Listen,' he says, close to your ear over the engines. 'When the ramp drops, you don't stop. Not for gear, not for nothing, not for me. You get to the shingle.' Somewhere ahead in the dark is France. H-Hour is 0630.

squad.name: Sgt. Frank Kowalski
squad.trait: Chicago steelworker, profane and gentle, memorizes everyone's hometown

squad.name: Cpl. Dee Ricks
squad.trait: Texan, cool under fire, awful poker player

squad.name: Pvt. Sal Marino
squad.trait: Brooklyn, fastest mouth in the company, scared of cows

squad.name: Pvt. Eugene 'Doc' Loomis
squad.trait: conscientious about everything, carries extra morphine and photographs

squad.name: Pvt. Henry Otoole
squad.trait: farm kid, fixes anything with wire and patience

timelinePressure: 1944-06-06 D-Day - Omaha Beach and the bluffs
1944-06-13 Into the hedgerow country - the bocage fight begins
1944-12-16 The Ardennes erupts - the Battle of the Bulge
